# Phase 1 — Foundations

**Prerequisite:** None. This is where you start.

This phase builds the mental models you need for everything else. Before writing a single line of HTML, you need to understand what actually happens when you type a URL into a browser. Most beginners skip this — don't. It makes every other topic click faster.

## Topics

| # | Topic | What You'll Learn |
|---|-------|-------------------|
| 01 | [How the Web Works](01-how-the-web-works.md) | DNS, clients, servers, the request/response cycle |
| 02 | [HTTP & Browsers](02-http-and-browsers.md) | HTTP methods, status codes, how browsers parse and render pages |
| 03 | [HTML Basics](03-html-basics.md) | Elements, attributes, document structure |
| 04 | [CSS Basics](04-css-basics.md) | Selectors, the box model, the cascade |
| 05 | [Dev Tools](05-dev-tools.md) | Chrome DevTools for inspecting, debugging, and experimenting |

## What's Next
